title: Fauxdori wraparound notebook cover
materials: veg-tan-leather-thin (1.2-1.5mm, 20cm × 30cm piece for an A5 cover)
round elastic cord (2mm, for holding inserts)
saddle soap or neatsfoot oil for finishing the leather (optional)
sources: SYNTHESISED — the Midori / Traveler's notebook style originated with Midori (Designphil, Japan) in 2006, copyright. The construction principle — a leather sheet folded around a spine with elastic inserts — is simple enough to synthesise from general leatherwork and bookbinding geometry principles.
notes: A wraparound leather notebook cover holding removable pamphlet-stitch inserts via elastic loops — the DIY version of the Traveler's notebook / Midori form factor. Covers: cutting the leather to size (cover panel + spine allowance + back panel, all one piece — no joining), marking and punching the spine holes, threading the elastic through, fitting standard A5 inserts (linked via elastic loops), optional dye and finish, edge burnishing. The whole cover is a single rectangle of leather — no sewing required on the cover itself. Distinguish from the travellers-notebook-leather-cover tutorial (which might use multiple pieces with stitching).
